lines.text: I'm positioned on the East Side near Schenk's Corners. {{ELAPSED}} into the Purge. Kill count is holding at 38 cumulative — that's 19 in this hour alone, up 11% from the same window last year.
Wind's shifted northwest, gusting hard now. Temperature's dropped to 33. I can see breath. Caroline was right about the timing on that cold front.
East Side is pulling 34% of the city's total activity. That's 13 kills in this neighborhood in the last 60 minutes. Atwood corridor especially. The data suggests coordinated movement — not random.

Weapons profile is shifting. Bladed implements are running about 28% higher than this point last year. Firearm usage down — ammo costs, like Holden said earlier. People are getting creative with what they have.
I'm moving south toward the residential blocks. Visibility is maybe 6 miles now, dropping. The wind is actually helping — most activity is concentrating on the leeward side of the—

tickerItems.text: Madison cumulative: 38 kills. YoY delta: +11% at this hour mark.
East Side (Atwood/Schenk's): 13 kills this hour. 34% of citywide total.
Cold front arrived on schedule. Wind gusts 20+ mph. Temperature 33F and dropping.
PurgeShield panels holding in most East Side installations. One reported structural failure on Atwood Ave — call 1-800-SHIELDME for emergency retrofit.
